articles.headline: National Education Day 2024: India Remembers Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, Focus on NEP Implementation
articles.explainer:
• India celebrates National Education Day on November 11th, commemorating the birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, the first Education Minister of independent India.
• The day highlights Azad's immense contributions to India's education system and his vision for universal education.
• Discussions focus on the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states.
• Government reiterates its commitment to foundational literacy, skill development, and integrating technology in education.
• Experts call for increased public and private investment in education infrastructure and teacher training.
gsSubTopics: Education
Human Resource Development
Government Policies and Interventions
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: National Education Day: November 11 (birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad).
Maulana Abul Kalam Azad: First Education Minister of India, Bharat Ratna recipient (posthumously in 1992).
NEP 2020: Focuses on universalization of education, multidisciplinary approach, vocational training, digital education.
Key initiatives under NEP: NIPUN Bharat Mission, PM e-Vidya, National Research Foundation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and vision of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad for India's education system.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in achieving equitable and quality education.
Discuss the role of education in nation-building, economic development, and social empowerment in contemporary India.
Suggest policy measures to address issues like digital divide, teacher shortages, and quality of learning outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad alongside students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the blend of legacy and future in Indian education.
articles.trivia: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad started the weekly journal 'Al-Hilal' in 1912 to propagate revolutionary ideas against British rule.

articles.headline: India's Economic Growth Trajectory: Q2 GDP Projections Point Towards Robust Recovery
articles.explainer:
• Economists and financial institutions are projecting a strong Q2 (July-September) GDP growth for India, buoyed by domestic demand.
• Key drivers include robust performance in manufacturing, services, and construction sectors, along with festive season spending.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) maintains its optimistic outlook, while also monitoring global economic headwinds and inflation.
• Government policy support, including capital expenditure and production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, is contributing significantly.
• Challenges persist from global supply chain disruptions, energy price volatility, and potential impact of El Niño on agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Q2 of Indian financial year: July to September.
GDP is calculated by National Statistical Office (NSO) under Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Key sectors contributing to India's GDP: Agriculture, Industry (Manufacturing, Mining, Construction), Services.
RBI's role in monetary policy to manage inflation and support growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's projected economic growth and its sustainability in the medium term.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties, inflation, and unemployment on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government fiscal policies and RBI's monetary policy in fostering economic recovery.
Examine the role of structural reforms (e.g., PLI schemes) in boosting manufacturing and exports.
articles.imageDescription: A graphical representation of economic growth trends with upward arrows and charts, symbolizing India's economic recovery.
articles.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

articles.headline: India Ramps Up Preparations for COP29, Reiterates Call for Climate Justice and Equity
articles.explainer:
• India is actively engaging in preparatory talks for the 29th Conference of the Parties (COP29) to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
• The nation continues to advocate strongly for 'climate justice' and 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC).
• Emphasis is placed on developed nations fulfilling their climate finance commitments and facilitating technology transfer to developing countries.
• India highlights its ambitious domestic climate action targets, including its updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• Discussions also cover strategies for global stocktake outcomes and mechanisms for loss and damage funding.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
articles.source: Ministry of Environment / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: COP29 is scheduled to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change) is the parent treaty for COPs.
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities) is a principle under UNFCCC.
India's updated NDCs include achieving about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's position on climate change negotiations, particularly its emphasis on climate justice and CBDR-RC.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and technology transfer for developing countries.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international climate agreements in achieving global emission reduction targets.
Examine India's domestic climate action strategies and their role in contributing to global climate goals.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with green energy symbols and a diverse group of people, representing international cooperation on climate change.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement, adopted at COP21 in 2015, aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

articles.headline: India Deepens Strategic Ties with Middle East Nations Amid Evolving Geopolitics
articles.explainer:
• India continues to strengthen its diplomatic, economic, and security partnerships with key Middle Eastern countries.
• This engagement is crucial for India's energy security, trade routes, and the welfare of its large diaspora in the region.
• Bilateral and multilateral dialogues focus on counter-terrorism, maritime security, and investment in infrastructure.
• India's 'Look West' policy aligns with its broader Indo-Pacific strategy, ensuring regional stability and connectivity.
• The I2U2 grouping (India, Israel, UAE, USA) is gaining momentum, fostering cooperation in water, energy, transport, space, health, and food security.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: I2U2 group includes India, Israel, UAE, and USA.
The Middle East is vital for India's crude oil imports and natural gas.
India has significant diaspora populations in Gulf countries.
Key initiatives include India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Middle East for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss how India balances its relationships with various regional powers in the Middle East amidst complex geopolitical dynamics.
Evaluate the potential of initiatives like I2U2 and IMEC in enhancing regional connectivity and economic cooperation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's energy security and diaspora welfare in the region.
articles.imageDescription: A map highlighting India and Middle Eastern countries with connecting trade routes, symbolizing strong bilateral ties.
articles.trivia: The India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in 2023.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Digital Public Infrastructure Initiatives for Enhanced Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government is assessing the impact and reach of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) initiatives, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
• DPI platforms have significantly improved financial inclusion, direct benefit transfers, and access to government services.
• Efforts are underway to expand these digital services to remote areas and ensure greater digital literacy among the populace.
• Challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and equitable access for all citizens remain key areas of focus.
• India aims to share its DPI model with other developing nations, promoting a global digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: E-governance, applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: India Stack comprises Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, and other digital platforms.
UPI is managed by National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
DigiLocker allows citizens to store and access digital documents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Digital Public Infrastructure in enhancing governance, financial inclusion, and service delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges associated with DPI implementation, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and the digital divide.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can be replicated or adapted by other developing countries.
Examine the role of DPI in achieving sustainable development goals and fostering a cashless economy.
articles.imageDescription: Digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, connected by lines, symbolizing integrated digital services.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: ISRO Proposes Advanced Strategies to Mitigate Growing Threat of Space Debris
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is intensifying its efforts to track and mitigate the increasing menace of space debris.
• This includes developing indigenous capabilities for Space Situational Awareness (SSA) and actively participating in international forums.
• ISRO is exploring technologies for active debris removal and designing satellites for de-orbiting at the end of their operational life.
• The growing number of satellite launches globally necessitates urgent measures to ensure the sustainability of outer space activities.
• Collaboration with global space agencies is crucial for developing a comprehensive framework for space traffic management.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's Project NETRA (Network for space object Tracking and Analysis) aims to track space debris.
Kessler Syndrome: A scenario where the density of objects in low Earth orbit (LEO) is high enough that collisions between objects could cause a cascade.
Outer Space Treaty (1967) governs activities in outer space.
GEO (Geosynchronous Earth Orbit) and LEO (Low Earth Orbit) are critical orbits for satellites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing threat posed by space debris to active satellites and future space missions.
Analyze the technological and policy initiatives undertaken by ISRO and India to address the space debris problem.
Evaluate the need for international cooperation and a global regulatory framework for space traffic management and debris mitigation.
Examine the strategic and economic implications of ensuring sustainable access to outer space.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of various satellites and fragments of space debris orbiting Earth, highlighting the congestion in space.
articles.trivia: The first piece of man-made space debris was the upper stage of the Sputnik 1 rocket, launched in 1957.

articles.headline: Union Ministry Launches New Initiatives for Comprehensive Elderly Care and Welfare
articles.explainer:
• Recognizing the demographic shift towards an aging population, the Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has unveiled new schemes.
• These initiatives focus on providing better healthcare access, financial security, and social integration for senior citizens.
• Programs aim to strengthen old age homes, expand geriatric care facilities, and promote active aging.
• Collaborations with NGOs and local communities are being encouraged to create a supportive ecosystem for the elderly.
• Addressing issues like loneliness, abuse, and digital literacy among seniors is a key objective.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by Centre and States
articles.source: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / PIB
keyTerms: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act
Senior Able Citizens for Re-employment in Dignity (SACRED)
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is the nodal ministry for elderly welfare.
India's demographic dividend is gradually shifting, with an increasing proportion of elderly.
Schemes like PM Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) provide social security benefits to seniors.
Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007, ensures legal protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by the aging population in India and the need for comprehensive welfare policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies in ensuring the well-being and dignity of senior citizens.
Examine the role of family, community, and civil society in providing support and care for the elderly.
Suggest innovative approaches to leverage the experience and knowledge of the elderly for national development.
articles.imageDescription: A group of happy senior citizens participating in community activities, symbolizing active and dignified aging.
articles.trivia: The global population aged 60 years or over is projected to double by 2050, reaching 2.1 billion.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Cyber Security Infrastructure Amid Rising Global Cyber Threats
articles.explainer:
• In response to an escalating global cyber threat landscape, India is significantly upgrading its cybersecurity infrastructure and protocols.
• The focus is on protecting critical national infrastructure, government networks, and citizen data from sophisticated attacks.
• The National Cyber Security Strategy is being recalibrated to incorporate emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing.
• Increased collaboration between government agencies, private sector, and international partners is emphasized.
• Initiatives include capacity building, skill development in cybersecurity, and public awareness campaigns on digital safety.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks
role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
basics of cyber security
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents.
NCIIPC protects critical information infrastructure (e.g., power, banking, telecom).
Data Protection Bill (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) is a key legislative framework.
Common cyber threats include phishing, ransomware, malware, and DDoS attacks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key pillars of India's cybersecurity strategy and the challenges in its effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Examine the balance between national security concerns and individual privacy rights in the context of cybersecurity laws.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, representing national cybersecurity protection.
articles.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Brain', was created in 1986 by two Pakistani brothers.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Emphasizes Need for Speedy Implementation of New Criminal Laws
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has underscored the urgency of implementing the recently enacted Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam.
• These new laws are set to replace the Indian Penal Code (IPC), Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), and Indian Evidence Act.
• The judiciary highlights the need for adequate training of law enforcement agencies, public prosecutors, and judicial officers.
• Focus is on ensuring a smooth transition and addressing potential challenges in infrastructure and technology.
• The reforms aim to modernize the criminal justice system, enhance efficiency, and ensure timely justice delivery.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
articles.source: Supreme Court / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita replaces IPC.
Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita replaces CrPC.
Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam replaces Indian Evidence Act.
Key changes include provisions for community service, electronic evidence, and forensic investigation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the overhaul of India's colonial-era criminal laws and the key objectives of the new legislations.
Analyze the potential impact of these new laws on the criminal justice system, including law enforcement, judiciary, and citizens' rights.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation, such as training, infrastructure, and public awareness.
Examine how these reforms aim to ensure speedy justice and victim-centric approaches.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books with the Indian flag in the background, symbolizing justice and legal reform.
articles.trivia: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) was enacted in 1860 during the British Raj.

articles.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Reviews Inflation Trajectory and Growth Prospects
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is closely monitoring the evolving inflation trajectory and its implications for economic growth.
• Recent spikes in food prices, particularly vegetables and cereals, continue to be a concern, alongside global commodity price volatility.
• The MPC's stance aims to balance supporting economic recovery while ensuring inflation remains within the target band (2-6%).
• Future interest rate decisions will depend on the sustained easing of inflationary pressures and global economic stability.
• Experts discuss the potential for a rate cut in the near future, contingent on favorable economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: MPC is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
CPI (Consumer Price Index) is the main measure of inflation for MPC's decisions.
Fiscal policy is managed by the government, while monetary policy is by RBI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Analyze the factors contributing to inflationary pressures in India, including supply-side and demand-side issues.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools (e.g., repo rate changes) in managing the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
articles.imageDescription: A magnifying glass examining a financial graph, symbolizing the RBI's close monitoring of economic indicators.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members (three from RBI, three external members).

articles.headline: Northern India Grapples with Deteriorating Air Quality; Urgent Measures Advocated
articles.explainer:
• Several cities in Northern India, particularly the Delhi-NCR region, are experiencing severe air quality degradation, prompting health advisories.
• The seasonal phenomenon is attributed to a combination of factors including stubble burning, vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and unfavorable meteorological conditions.
• Government agencies are implementing the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) to mitigate pollution levels.
• Calls for long-term, sustainable solutions, including promoting public transport, renewable energy, and crop residue management, are intensifying.
• Public awareness and inter-state cooperation are crucial for effective control and prevention of air pollution.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
keyTerms: System of Air Quality and Weather Forecasting and Research (SAFAR)
prelimsPoints: AQI (Air Quality Index) measures air quality based on several pollutants (PM2.5, PM10, SO2, NO2, CO, O3, NH3, Pb).
PM2.5 refers to particulate matter smaller than 2.5 micrometers, which can penetrate deep into lungs.
GRAP is a set of emergency measures implemented based on AQI levels in Delhi-NCR.
Stubble burning in Punjab and Haryana is a major contributor to winter pollution in Delhi.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Northern India, particularly during winter months.
Discuss the health, environmental, and economic impacts of severe air pollution on urban populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like GRAP and other long-term strategies in mitigating air pollution.
Propose comprehensive solutions involving inter-state cooperation, technological interventions, and behavioral changes to address this perennial crisis.
articles.imageDescription: A smog-filled cityscape of Delhi, with people wearing masks, illustrating the severe air pollution.
articles.trivia: Air pollution is estimated to cause millions of premature deaths worldwide each year, with a significant burden on India.

articles.headline: India Champions Voice of Global South at International Fora for Equitable Development
articles.explainer:
• India continues to assert its leadership in representing the interests of the Global South on critical international platforms like G20 and BRICS.
• The focus is on advocating for equitable global governance, climate finance, debt relief, and accessible technology for developing nations.
• India emphasizes South-South cooperation and sharing its development model and digital public infrastructure (DPI) with partner countries.
• The discussions highlight challenges such as food and energy security, sustainable development, and geopolitical stability.
• India's approach aims to foster a more inclusive and representative international order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora
India and its neighborhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa) is an economic bloc.
India advocates for reforms in global financial institutions like the IMF and World Bank.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South in contemporary international relations.
Discuss the key issues and challenges faced by developing countries that India champions on global platforms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like G20 and BRICS in promoting equitable global development.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes South-South cooperation and its impact on regional and global dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: A world map with specific regions highlighted, symbolizing the 'Global South' and India's leadership.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' emerged in the late 20th century as a way to refer to socio-economically developing countries.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Finds Shed Light on Ancient Indian Civilizations
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in various parts of India have unearthed significant artifacts, providing fresh insights into ancient civilizations.
• Discoveries include remnants of ancient settlements, pottery, tools, and inscriptions, dating back several millennia.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of historical trade routes, social structures, and cultural practices.
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is leading efforts in conservation and further research.
• The discoveries underscore the rich and diverse heritage of India, offering new perspectives for historical studies.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and heritage preservation in India.
Carbon dating is a common method used to determine the age of organic materials.
Key ancient civilizations: Indus Valley (Harappan), Vedic period, Mahajanapadas.
Epigraphy is the study of inscriptions, and numismatics is the study of coins.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing historical sites.
Examine the role of archaeological research in promoting cultural tourism and national identity.
Suggest measures for public engagement and community participation in heritage preservation.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments and structural remains.
articles.trivia: The Harappan civilization, one of the world's earliest urban civilizations, flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.

articles.headline: Skill India Mission Boosts Youth Employability with Focus on Emerging Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The Skill India Mission is actively working to enhance the employability of India's youth by aligning training programs with industry demands.
• A renewed emphasis is placed on future-ready skills, including artificial intelligence, data science, coding, and green jobs.
• The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) and Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) are instrumental in delivering vocational training.
• Partnerships with private sector companies and international organizations are being forged to ensure relevant curriculum and placement opportunities.
• The mission aims to address the skill gap in India's workforce and foster entrepreneurship among young individuals.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Human Resource Development
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development / The Hindu BusinessLine
prelimsPoints: Skill India Mission was launched in 2015.
PMKVY is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
NSDC (National Skill Development Corporation) is a not-for-profit public limited company under the Ministry.
ITIs provide vocational training in various trades.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in leveraging India's demographic dividend and boosting economic growth.
Analyze the challenges in matching skills with industry demands and the effectiveness of current skill development initiatives.
Evaluate the role of emerging technologies in shaping the future of work and the need for upskilling and reskilling programs.
Examine how entrepreneurship development can be integrated with skill training to create self-employment opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: Young individuals learning vocational skills in a modern training center, focusing on digital tools.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, with over 65% of its population below 35 years of age.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Healthcare Digitization
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported substantial progress in creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
• Key milestones include the generation of millions of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) and the registration of numerous health facilities.
• The mission aims to link patients' digital health records across various providers, improving accessibility and efficiency of care.
• While expanding reach, the government is also addressing concerns related to data privacy, security, and interoperability.
• ABDM is a crucial step towards achieving universal health coverage and a more patient-centric healthcare system in India.
gsSubTopics: Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
E-governance, applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
articles.source: National Health Authority / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
ABHA ID is a unique 14-digit health ID for every citizen.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM and Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY).
It aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the key components and achievements of ABDM, including ABHA IDs and digital health records.
Evaluate the challenges related to data privacy, interoperability, digital literacy, and infrastructure in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem.
Examine how ABDM contributes to achieving universal health coverage and a more equitable healthcare system.
articles.imageDescription: Digital network lines connecting health icons (heart, cross), symbolizing integrated digital healthcare services.
articles.trivia: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.

articles.headline: Editorial: Strengthening Cooperative Federalism for India's Sustainable Growth
articles.explainer:
• The editorial argues for a renewed focus on cooperative federalism to address India's complex development challenges.
• It highlights the importance of harmonious Centre-state relations in policy formulation, resource allocation, and implementation of welfare schemes.
• The role of institutions like the GST Council and Finance Commission in fostering fiscal federalism is critically examined.
• Challenges such as inter-state disputes, political divergences, and uneven development necessitate greater collaboration.
• A strong cooperative federal structure is essential for achieving sustainable economic growth and inclusive social development across diverse regions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Cooperative federalism emphasizes cooperation between Centre and states.
GST Council is a constitutional body for Goods and Services Tax.
Finance Commission recommends distribution of taxes between Union and states (Article 280).
Seventh Schedule of the Constitution divides powers between Union and states.
mainsPoints: Define and differentiate between cooperative and competitive federalism, providing examples from the Indian context.
Analyze the constitutional provisions and institutional mechanisms (e.g., Finance Commission, GST Council) that facilitate Centre-state relations in India.
Discuss the major challenges to cooperative federalism, such as fiscal imbalances, political differences, and inter-state disputes.
Suggest reforms and measures to strengthen cooperative federalism for more effective governance and equitable development in India.
articles.imageDescription: Two hands shaking over a map of India, symbolizing cooperation between central and state governments.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'cooperative federalism' gained prominence in India, particularly with the establishment of NITI Aayog replacing the Planning Commission.

articles.headline: Government Emphasizes Gender Budgeting for Empowering Women and Girls
articles.explainer:
• The government highlights the importance of gender budgeting as a tool for promoting gender equality and women's empowerment.
• This involves analyzing the budget from a gender perspective to assess its impact on women and girls.
• Initiatives focus on allocating resources for education, health, skill development, and economic opportunities for women.
• Efforts are being made to increase women's participation in the workforce and decision-making roles.
• The aim is to bridge gender gaps and ensure that development benefits reach all sections of society equitably.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
social empowerment
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
articles.source: Ministry of Women & Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: Gender Budgeting is not a separate budget but an analysis of government budget from a gender perspective.
It was first introduced in India in 2005-06.
Ministry of Women and Child Development is the nodal ministry for gender-related policies.
SDG 5 focuses on achieving gender equality and empowering all women and girls.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of gender budgeting and its significance in promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing effective gender budgeting and ensuring its impact on women's socio-economic status.
Evaluate the role of various government schemes and policies aimed at women's development and protection.
Suggest measures to enhance women's participation in the economy and decision-making processes.
articles.imageDescription: A balance scale with symbols of men and women, depicting the goal of gender equality through equitable resource allocation.
articles.trivia: Australia was one of the first countries to implement gender budgeting in the 1980s.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Renewable Energy Targets, Focus on Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• India is pushing aggressively to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Significant investments are being channeled into solar, wind, and hydropower projects across the country.
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is a key strategic initiative to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Policy support, including incentives and regulatory frameworks, is being strengthened to attract private sector participation.
• This transition is crucial for India's energy security, climate commitments, and fostering a green economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Energy
Infrastructure
articles.source: Ministry of New & Renewable Energy / Mint
prelimsPoints: India's target for non-fossil fuel energy capacity is 500 GW by 2030.
National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) production by 2030.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
REWA Ultra Mega Solar Park is one of the largest single-site solar power plants in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's renewable energy targets and the progress made towards achieving them, highlighting key government initiatives.
Analyze the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and global climate leadership.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the renewable energy transition, including financing, technology, and grid integration.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental benefits of shifting towards a green economy in India.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines against a clear sky, with a hydrogen molecule symbol, representing renewable energy and green hydrogen.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world.

highlights: National Education Day observed, commemorating Maulana Abul Kalam Azad's birth anniversary; NEP 2020 implementation reviewed.
India's Q2 GDP growth projections indicate robust economic recovery, driven by manufacturing and services sectors.
India actively prepares for COP29, reiterating its call for climate justice, finance, and technology transfer for developing nations.
Government reviews progress of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) initiatives like Aadhaar and UPI for enhanced service delivery.
Supreme Court emphasizes speedy implementation of new criminal laws (Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, etc.) to modernize justice system.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to National Education Day in India, consider the following statements:
1. It is celebrated on November 11th every year to commemorate the birth anniversary of Dr. S. Radhakrishnan.
2. Maulana Abul Kalam Azad served as the first Education Minister of independent India.
3. The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to universalize education from pre-school to secondary level by 2030.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation:
Statement 1 is incorrect: National Education Day is celebrated on November 11th to commemorate the birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, not Dr. S. Radhakrishnan (whose birth anniversary on September 5th is celebrated as Teachers' Day).
Statement 2 is correct: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad was indeed the first Education Minister of independent India.
Statement 3 is correct: A key aim of the NEP 2020 is to universalize education from pre-school to secondary level with 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in school education by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth. Ministry of Finance deals with fiscal policy, SEBI regulates capital markets, and NITI Aayog is a policy think tank.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in India:
1. Aadhaar provides a unique 12-digit identification number to all Indian residents.
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is operated by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
3. DigiLocker allows citizens to securely store and access digital documents and certificates.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI. UPI is a system that powers multiple bank accounts into a single mobile application, merging several banking features, seamless fund routing & merchant payments into one hood, and is operated by NPCI. DigiLocker is a flagship initiative under the Digital India program, providing a secure cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents & certificates.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents when they occur in India?
options: (a) National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for performing the functions of incident response, vulnerability analysis, and security quality management in the field of cybersecurity. NCIIPC protects critical information infrastructure. NTRO is a technical intelligence agency. Cyber Swachhta Kendra is a botnet cleaning and malware analysis centre.

prelimsMocks.question: The principle of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) is primarily associated with which of the following international agreements/conventions?
options: (c) United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The principle of CBDR-RC is a foundational principle of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). It recognizes that all countries have a shared responsibility to address climate change but acknowledges their different capabilities and historical contributions to the problem, thus requiring differentiated responsibilities, especially between developed and developing nations.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. Discuss how India can leverage its demographic dividend through effective educational reforms.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NEP 2020 and its objectives. Discuss key achievements (e.g., curriculum reforms, digital initiatives). Analyze challenges (e.g., funding, infrastructure, teacher training, digital divide, state-level implementation). Connect education reform with demographic dividend, emphasizing skill development, vocational education, and research. Conclude with recommendations for holistic development.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the role of cooperative federalism in addressing India's complex development challenges. Discuss the institutional mechanisms that facilitate Centre-state relations and suggest measures to strengthen them.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Define cooperative federalism and its importance in India's diverse context. Discuss how it helps in policy implementation and resource mobilization. Analyze constitutional provisions (e.g., Seventh Schedule) and institutions (e.g., Finance Commission, NITI Aayog, GST Council, Inter-State Council). Highlight challenges like fiscal imbalances, political differences, and inter-state disputes. Conclude with suggestions for reforms like greater consultation, financial autonomy, and dispute resolution mechanisms.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the increasing threat posed by space debris to active satellites and future space missions. What measures is India undertaking to mitigate this challenge, and what role can international cooperation play?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the problem of space debris (Kessler Syndrome, economic and strategic threats). Explain India's efforts: ISRO's SSA capabilities (NETRA), designing satellites for de-orbiting, exploring active debris removal. Discuss the necessity of international cooperation: global regulatory frameworks, data sharing, space traffic management, and adherence to guidelines (e.g., UNCOPUOS). Conclude on ensuring sustainable use of outer space.

quickBytes.text: National Education Day is celebrated on Nov 11, marking Maulana Abul Kalam Azad's birth anniversary.
NEP 2020 aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio in school education by 2030.
Q2 (July-Sept) GDP data is crucial for assessing India's economic health and recovery.
COP29 is slated to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan, focusing on global climate action.
I2U2 grouping includes India, Israel, UAE, and USA, focusing on economic cooperation.
UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker form key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure.
ISRO's Project NETRA is dedicated to Space Situational Awareness and debris tracking.
Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita replaces the Indian Penal Code (IPC).
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's benchmark interest rate (repo rate).
ABHA ID (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a 14-digit unique health ID.

GS1.headline: Union Ministry Launches New Initiatives for Comprehensive Elderly Care and Welfare
GS1.explainer:
• Recognizing the demographic shift towards an aging population, the Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has unveiled new schemes.
• These initiatives focus on providing better healthcare access, financial security, and social integration for senior citizens.
• Programs aim to strengthen old age homes, expand geriatric care facilities, and promote active aging.
• Collaborations with NGOs and local communities are being encouraged to create a supportive ecosystem for the elderly.
• Addressing issues like loneliness, abuse, and digital literacy among seniors is a key objective.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by Centre and States
GS1.source: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / PIB
keyTerms: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act
Senior Able Citizens for Re-employment in Dignity (SACRED)
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is the nodal ministry for elderly welfare.
India's demographic dividend is gradually shifting, with an increasing proportion of elderly.
Schemes like PM Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) provide social security benefits to seniors.
Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007, ensures legal protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by the aging population in India and the need for comprehensive welfare policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies in ensuring the well-being and dignity of senior citizens.
Examine the role of family, community, and civil society in providing support and care for the elderly.
Suggest innovative approaches to leverage the experience and knowledge of the elderly for national development.
GS1.imageDescription: A group of happy senior citizens participating in community activities, symbolizing active and dignified aging.
GS1.trivia: The global population aged 60 years or over is projected to double by 2050, reaching 2.1 billion.

GS1.headline: New Archaeological Finds Shed Light on Ancient Indian Civilizations
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in various parts of India have unearthed significant artifacts, providing fresh insights into ancient civilizations.
• Discoveries include remnants of ancient settlements, pottery, tools, and inscriptions, dating back several millennia.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of historical trade routes, social structures, and cultural practices.
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is leading efforts in conservation and further research.
• The discoveries underscore the rich and diverse heritage of India, offering new perspectives for historical studies.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and heritage preservation in India.
Carbon dating is a common method used to determine the age of organic materials.
Key ancient civilizations: Indus Valley (Harappan), Vedic period, Mahajanapadas.
Epigraphy is the study of inscriptions, and numismatics is the study of coins.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing historical sites.
Examine the role of archaeological research in promoting cultural tourism and national identity.
Suggest measures for public engagement and community participation in heritage preservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments and structural remains.
GS1.trivia: The Harappan civilization, one of the world's earliest urban civilizations, flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.

GS1.headline: Government Emphasizes Gender Budgeting for Empowering Women and Girls
GS1.explainer:
• The government highlights the importance of gender budgeting as a tool for promoting gender equality and women's empowerment.
• This involves analyzing the budget from a gender perspective to assess its impact on women and girls.
• Initiatives focus on allocating resources for education, health, skill development, and economic opportunities for women.
• Efforts are being made to increase women's participation in the workforce and decision-making roles.
• The aim is to bridge gender gaps and ensure that development benefits reach all sections of society equitably.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
social empowerment
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
GS1.source: Ministry of Women & Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: Gender Budgeting is not a separate budget but an analysis of government budget from a gender perspective.
It was first introduced in India in 2005-06.
Ministry of Women and Child Development is the nodal ministry for gender-related policies.
SDG 5 focuses on achieving gender equality and empowering all women and girls.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of gender budgeting and its significance in promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing effective gender budgeting and ensuring its impact on women's socio-economic status.
Evaluate the role of various government schemes and policies aimed at women's development and protection.
Suggest measures to enhance women's participation in the economy and decision-making processes.
GS1.imageDescription: A balance scale with symbols of men and women, depicting the goal of gender equality through equitable resource allocation.
GS1.trivia: Australia was one of the first countries to implement gender budgeting in the 1980s.

GS2.headline: National Education Day 2024: India Remembers Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, Focus on NEP Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• India celebrates National Education Day on November 11th, commemorating the birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, the first Education Minister of independent India.
• The day highlights Azad's immense contributions to India's education system and his vision for universal education.
• Discussions focus on the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states.
• Government reiterates its commitment to foundational literacy, skill development, and integrating technology in education.
• Experts call for increased public and private investment in education infrastructure and teacher training.
gsSubTopics: Education
Human Resource Development
Government Policies and Interventions
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: National Education Day: November 11 (birth anniversary of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad).
Maulana Abul Kalam Azad: First Education Minister of India, Bharat Ratna recipient (posthumously in 1992).
NEP 2020: Focuses on universalization of education, multidisciplinary approach, vocational training, digital education.
Key initiatives under NEP: NIPUN Bharat Mission, PM e-Vidya, National Research Foundation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and vision of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad for India's education system.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in achieving equitable and quality education.
Discuss the role of education in nation-building, economic development, and social empowerment in contemporary India.
Suggest policy measures to address issues like digital divide, teacher shortages, and quality of learning outcomes.
GS2.imageDescription: Portrait of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad alongside students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the blend of legacy and future in Indian education.
GS2.trivia: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad started the weekly journal 'Al-Hilal' in 1912 to propagate revolutionary ideas against British rule.

GS2.headline: India Deepens Strategic Ties with Middle East Nations Amid Evolving Geopolitics
GS2.explainer:
• India continues to strengthen its diplomatic, economic, and security partnerships with key Middle Eastern countries.
• This engagement is crucial for India's energy security, trade routes, and the welfare of its large diaspora in the region.
• Bilateral and multilateral dialogues focus on counter-terrorism, maritime security, and investment in infrastructure.
• India's 'Look West' policy aligns with its broader Indo-Pacific strategy, ensuring regional stability and connectivity.
• The I2U2 grouping (India, Israel, UAE, USA) is gaining momentum, fostering cooperation in water, energy, transport, space, health, and food security.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: I2U2 group includes India, Israel, UAE, and USA.
The Middle East is vital for India's crude oil imports and natural gas.
India has significant diaspora populations in Gulf countries.
Key initiatives include India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Middle East for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss how India balances its relationships with various regional powers in the Middle East amidst complex geopolitical dynamics.
Evaluate the potential of initiatives like I2U2 and IMEC in enhancing regional connectivity and economic cooperation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's energy security and diaspora welfare in the region.
GS2.imageDescription: A map highlighting India and Middle Eastern countries with connecting trade routes, symbolizing strong bilateral ties.
GS2.trivia: The India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in 2023.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Digital Public Infrastructure Initiatives for Enhanced Service Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian government is assessing the impact and reach of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) initiatives, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
• DPI platforms have significantly improved financial inclusion, direct benefit transfers, and access to government services.
• Efforts are underway to expand these digital services to remote areas and ensure greater digital literacy among the populace.
• Challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and equitable access for all citizens remain key areas of focus.
• India aims to share its DPI model with other developing nations, promoting a global digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: E-governance, applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: India Stack comprises Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, and other digital platforms.
UPI is managed by National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
DigiLocker allows citizens to store and access digital documents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Digital Public Infrastructure in enhancing governance, financial inclusion, and service delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges associated with DPI implementation, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and the digital divide.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can be replicated or adapted by other developing countries.
Examine the role of DPI in achieving sustainable development goals and fostering a cashless economy.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, connected by lines, symbolizing integrated digital services.
GS2.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Emphasizes Need for Speedy Implementation of New Criminal Laws
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has underscored the urgency of implementing the recently enacted Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam.
• These new laws are set to replace the Indian Penal Code (IPC), Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), and Indian Evidence Act.
• The judiciary highlights the need for adequate training of law enforcement agencies, public prosecutors, and judicial officers.
• Focus is on ensuring a smooth transition and addressing potential challenges in infrastructure and technology.
• The reforms aim to modernize the criminal justice system, enhance efficiency, and ensure timely justice delivery.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
GS2.source: Supreme Court / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita replaces IPC.
Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita replaces CrPC.
Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam replaces Indian Evidence Act.
Key changes include provisions for community service, electronic evidence, and forensic investigation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the overhaul of India's colonial-era criminal laws and the key objectives of the new legislations.
Analyze the potential impact of these new laws on the criminal justice system, including law enforcement, judiciary, and citizens' rights.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation, such as training, infrastructure, and public awareness.
Examine how these reforms aim to ensure speedy justice and victim-centric approaches.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and law books with the Indian flag in the background, symbolizing justice and legal reform.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) was enacted in 1860 during the British Raj.

GS2.headline: India Champions Voice of Global South at International Fora for Equitable Development
GS2.explainer:
• India continues to assert its leadership in representing the interests of the Global South on critical international platforms like G20 and BRICS.
• The focus is on advocating for equitable global governance, climate finance, debt relief, and accessible technology for developing nations.
• India emphasizes South-South cooperation and sharing its development model and digital public infrastructure (DPI) with partner countries.
• The discussions highlight challenges such as food and energy security, sustainable development, and geopolitical stability.
• India's approach aims to foster a more inclusive and representative international order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora
India and its neighborhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa) is an economic bloc.
India advocates for reforms in global financial institutions like the IMF and World Bank.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South in contemporary international relations.
Discuss the key issues and challenges faced by developing countries that India champions on global platforms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like G20 and BRICS in promoting equitable global development.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes South-South cooperation and its impact on regional and global dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: A world map with specific regions highlighted, symbolizing the 'Global South' and India's leadership.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' emerged in the late 20th century as a way to refer to socio-economically developing countries.

GS2.headline: Skill India Mission Boosts Youth Employability with Focus on Emerging Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• The Skill India Mission is actively working to enhance the employability of India's youth by aligning training programs with industry demands.
• A renewed emphasis is placed on future-ready skills, including artificial intelligence, data science, coding, and green jobs.
• The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) and Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) are instrumental in delivering vocational training.
• Partnerships with private sector companies and international organizations are being forged to ensure relevant curriculum and placement opportunities.
• The mission aims to address the skill gap in India's workforce and foster entrepreneurship among young individuals.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Human Resource Development
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development / The Hindu BusinessLine
prelimsPoints: Skill India Mission was launched in 2015.
PMKVY is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
NSDC (National Skill Development Corporation) is a not-for-profit public limited company under the Ministry.
ITIs provide vocational training in various trades.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in leveraging India's demographic dividend and boosting economic growth.
Analyze the challenges in matching skills with industry demands and the effectiveness of current skill development initiatives.
Evaluate the role of emerging technologies in shaping the future of work and the need for upskilling and reskilling programs.
Examine how entrepreneurship development can be integrated with skill training to create self-employment opportunities.
GS2.imageDescription: Young individuals learning vocational skills in a modern training center, focusing on digital tools.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, with over 65% of its population below 35 years of age.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Healthcare Digitization
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has reported substantial progress in creating a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
• Key milestones include the generation of millions of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) and the registration of numerous health facilities.
• The mission aims to link patients' digital health records across various providers, improving accessibility and efficiency of care.
• While expanding reach, the government is also addressing concerns related to data privacy, security, and interoperability.
• ABDM is a crucial step towards achieving universal health coverage and a more patient-centric healthcare system in India.
gsSubTopics: Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
E-governance, applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
GS2.source: National Health Authority / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
ABHA ID is a unique 14-digit health ID for every citizen.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM and Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY).
It aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the key components and achievements of ABDM, including ABHA IDs and digital health records.
Evaluate the challenges related to data privacy, interoperability, digital literacy, and infrastructure in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem.
Examine how ABDM contributes to achieving universal health coverage and a more equitable healthcare system.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital network lines connecting health icons (heart, cross), symbolizing integrated digital healthcare services.
GS2.trivia: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.

GS2.headline: Editorial: Strengthening Cooperative Federalism for India's Sustainable Growth
GS2.explainer:
• The editorial argues for a renewed focus on cooperative federalism to address India's complex development challenges.
• It highlights the importance of harmonious Centre-state relations in policy formulation, resource allocation, and implementation of welfare schemes.
• The role of institutions like the GST Council and Finance Commission in fostering fiscal federalism is critically examined.
• Challenges such as inter-state disputes, political divergences, and uneven development necessitate greater collaboration.
• A strong cooperative federal structure is essential for achieving sustainable economic growth and inclusive social development across diverse regions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Cooperative federalism emphasizes cooperation between Centre and states.
GST Council is a constitutional body for Goods and Services Tax.
Finance Commission recommends distribution of taxes between Union and states (Article 280).
Seventh Schedule of the Constitution divides powers between Union and states.
mainsPoints: Define and differentiate between cooperative and competitive federalism, providing examples from the Indian context.
Analyze the constitutional provisions and institutional mechanisms (e.g., Finance Commission, GST Council) that facilitate Centre-state relations in India.
Discuss the major challenges to cooperative federalism, such as fiscal imbalances, political differences, and inter-state disputes.
Suggest reforms and measures to strengthen cooperative federalism for more effective governance and equitable development in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Two hands shaking over a map of India, symbolizing cooperation between central and state governments.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'cooperative federalism' gained prominence in India, particularly with the establishment of NITI Aayog replacing the Planning Commission.

GS3.headline: India's Economic Growth Trajectory: Q2 GDP Projections Point Towards Robust Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• Economists and financial institutions are projecting a strong Q2 (July-September) GDP growth for India, buoyed by domestic demand.
• Key drivers include robust performance in manufacturing, services, and construction sectors, along with festive season spending.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) maintains its optimistic outlook, while also monitoring global economic headwinds and inflation.
• Government policy support, including capital expenditure and production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, is contributing significantly.
• Challenges persist from global supply chain disruptions, energy price volatility, and potential impact of El Niño on agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Q2 of Indian financial year: July to September.
GDP is calculated by National Statistical Office (NSO) under Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Key sectors contributing to India's GDP: Agriculture, Industry (Manufacturing, Mining, Construction), Services.
RBI's role in monetary policy to manage inflation and support growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's projected economic growth and its sustainability in the medium term.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties, inflation, and unemployment on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government fiscal policies and RBI's monetary policy in fostering economic recovery.
Examine the role of structural reforms (e.g., PLI schemes) in boosting manufacturing and exports.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphical representation of economic growth trends with upward arrows and charts, symbolizing India's economic recovery.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal GDP and the third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

GS3.headline: India Ramps Up Preparations for COP29, Reiterates Call for Climate Justice and Equity
GS3.explainer:
• India is actively engaging in preparatory talks for the 29th Conference of the Parties (COP29) to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
• The nation continues to advocate strongly for 'climate justice' and 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC).
• Emphasis is placed on developed nations fulfilling their climate finance commitments and facilitating technology transfer to developing countries.
• India highlights its ambitious domestic climate action targets, including its updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• Discussions also cover strategies for global stocktake outcomes and mechanisms for loss and damage funding.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: COP29 is scheduled to be held in Baku, Azerbaijan.
UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change) is the parent treaty for COPs.
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities) is a principle under UNFCCC.
India's updated NDCs include achieving about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's position on climate change negotiations, particularly its emphasis on climate justice and CBDR-RC.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and technology transfer for developing countries.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international climate agreements in achieving global emission reduction targets.
Examine India's domestic climate action strategies and their role in contributing to global climate goals.
GS3.imageDescription: A globe with green energy symbols and a diverse group of people, representing international cooperation on climate change.
GS3.trivia: The Paris Agreement, adopted at COP21 in 2015, aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS3.headline: ISRO Proposes Advanced Strategies to Mitigate Growing Threat of Space Debris
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is intensifying its efforts to track and mitigate the increasing menace of space debris.
• This includes developing indigenous capabilities for Space Situational Awareness (SSA) and actively participating in international forums.
• ISRO is exploring technologies for active debris removal and designing satellites for de-orbiting at the end of their operational life.
• The growing number of satellite launches globally necessitates urgent measures to ensure the sustainability of outer space activities.
• Collaboration with global space agencies is crucial for developing a comprehensive framework for space traffic management.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's Project NETRA (Network for space object Tracking and Analysis) aims to track space debris.
Kessler Syndrome: A scenario where the density of objects in low Earth orbit (LEO) is high enough that collisions between objects could cause a cascade.
Outer Space Treaty (1967) governs activities in outer space.
GEO (Geosynchronous Earth Orbit) and LEO (Low Earth Orbit) are critical orbits for satellites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing threat posed by space debris to active satellites and future space missions.
Analyze the technological and policy initiatives undertaken by ISRO and India to address the space debris problem.
Evaluate the need for international cooperation and a global regulatory framework for space traffic management and debris mitigation.
Examine the strategic and economic implications of ensuring sustainable access to outer space.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of various satellites and fragments of space debris orbiting Earth, highlighting the congestion in space.
GS3.trivia: The first piece of man-made space debris was the upper stage of the Sputnik 1 rocket, launched in 1957.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Cyber Security Infrastructure Amid Rising Global Cyber Threats
GS3.explainer:
• In response to an escalating global cyber threat landscape, India is significantly upgrading its cybersecurity infrastructure and protocols.
• The focus is on protecting critical national infrastructure, government networks, and citizen data from sophisticated attacks.
• The National Cyber Security Strategy is being recalibrated to incorporate emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing.
• Increased collaboration between government agencies, private sector, and international partners is emphasized.
• Initiatives include capacity building, skill development in cybersecurity, and public awareness campaigns on digital safety.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks
role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
basics of cyber security
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents.
NCIIPC protects critical information infrastructure (e.g., power, banking, telecom).
Data Protection Bill (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) is a key legislative framework.
Common cyber threats include phishing, ransomware, malware, and DDoS attacks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key pillars of India's cybersecurity strategy and the challenges in its effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Examine the balance between national security concerns and individual privacy rights in the context of cybersecurity laws.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, representing national cybersecurity protection.
GS3.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Brain', was created in 1986 by two Pakistani brothers.

GS3.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Reviews Inflation Trajectory and Growth Prospects
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is closely monitoring the evolving inflation trajectory and its implications for economic growth.
• Recent spikes in food prices, particularly vegetables and cereals, continue to be a concern, alongside global commodity price volatility.
• The MPC's stance aims to balance supporting economic recovery while ensuring inflation remains within the target band (2-6%).
• Future interest rate decisions will depend on the sustained easing of inflationary pressures and global economic stability.
• Experts discuss the potential for a rate cut in the near future, contingent on favorable economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: MPC is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
CPI (Consumer Price Index) is the main measure of inflation for MPC's decisions.
Fiscal policy is managed by the government, while monetary policy is by RBI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Analyze the factors contributing to inflationary pressures in India, including supply-side and demand-side issues.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools (e.g., repo rate changes) in managing the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: A magnifying glass examining a financial graph, symbolizing the RBI's close monitoring of economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members (three from RBI, three external members).

GS3.headline: Northern India Grapples with Deteriorating Air Quality; Urgent Measures Advocated
GS3.explainer:
• Several cities in Northern India, particularly the Delhi-NCR region, are experiencing severe air quality degradation, prompting health advisories.
• The seasonal phenomenon is attributed to a combination of factors including stubble burning, vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and unfavorable meteorological conditions.
• Government agencies are implementing the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) to mitigate pollution levels.
• Calls for long-term, sustainable solutions, including promoting public transport, renewable energy, and crop residue management, are intensifying.
• Public awareness and inter-state cooperation are crucial for effective control and prevention of air pollution.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
keyTerms: System of Air Quality and Weather Forecasting and Research (SAFAR)
prelimsPoints: AQI (Air Quality Index) measures air quality based on several pollutants (PM2.5, PM10, SO2, NO2, CO, O3, NH3, Pb).
PM2.5 refers to particulate matter smaller than 2.5 micrometers, which can penetrate deep into lungs.
GRAP is a set of emergency measures implemented based on AQI levels in Delhi-NCR.
Stubble burning in Punjab and Haryana is a major contributor to winter pollution in Delhi.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Northern India, particularly during winter months.
Discuss the health, environmental, and economic impacts of severe air pollution on urban populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like GRAP and other long-term strategies in mitigating air pollution.
Propose comprehensive solutions involving inter-state cooperation, technological interventions, and behavioral changes to address this perennial crisis.
GS3.imageDescription: A smog-filled cityscape of Delhi, with people wearing masks, illustrating the severe air pollution.
GS3.trivia: Air pollution is estimated to cause millions of premature deaths worldwide each year, with a significant burden on India.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Renewable Energy Targets, Focus on Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• India is pushing aggressively to achieve its ambitious renewable energy targets, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Significant investments are being channeled into solar, wind, and hydropower projects across the country.
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is a key strategic initiative to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• Policy support, including incentives and regulatory frameworks, is being strengthened to attract private sector participation.
• This transition is crucial for India's energy security, climate commitments, and fostering a green economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Energy
Infrastructure
GS3.source: Ministry of New & Renewable Energy / Mint
prelimsPoints: India's target for non-fossil fuel energy capacity is 500 GW by 2030.
National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) production by 2030.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
REWA Ultra Mega Solar Park is one of the largest single-site solar power plants in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's renewable energy targets and the progress made towards achieving them, highlighting key government initiatives.
Analyze the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and global climate leadership.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the renewable energy transition, including financing, technology, and grid integration.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental benefits of shifting towards a green economy in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines against a clear sky, with a hydrogen molecule symbol, representing renewable energy and green hydrogen.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world.

sources: Ministry of Environment / Down To Earth
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / PIB
